name: Agentic AI
summary: Agentic AI represents the critical transition from passive, conversational AI models to autonomous systems capable of executing multi-step workflows, utilizing external tools, and making decisions within defined boundaries. While current headlines are dominated by geopolitical noise—such as Iran negotiations, Middle East oil flows, and Brazilian elections—the underlying technological substrate for Agentic AI is rapidly maturing beneath the surface. The market context reflects this ongoing tech buildout, with strength in semiconductor (SMH) and robotics (BOTZ) ETFs highlighting the hardware backbone required for this computational shift. As inference costs decline and function-calling capabilities improve, Agentic AI is poised to disrupt enterprise software, evolving traditional 'Software-as-a-Service' into 'Service-as-Software' where AI delivers the completed outcome rather than just the tool.
key_drivers: Maturation of function calling and tool-use capabilities in foundational models.
Decreasing inference costs enabling longer, economically viable multi-step autonomous task execution.
Enterprise demand for workflow automation, cost reduction, and bridging legacy software silos.
Development of multi-agent orchestration frameworks and open-source ecosystems.
Hardware infrastructure buildout (evidenced by SMH strength) supporting complex, agentic compute loads.
Shift from 'Software as a Service' to 'Service as Software' where AI agents are hired to perform specific roles.
Integration of AI into physical robotics (BOTZ) bridging digital agents and real-world physical action.
Standardization of API ecosystems allowing AI agents to reliably interact with external digital environments.

adoption_curve.growth_rate: Exponential (>100% YoY in developer tooling and pilot enterprise deployments)
inflection_points: Achievement of reliable, self-correcting multi-step reasoning without human-in-the-loop intervention.
Establishment of enterprise security, liability, and governance frameworks for autonomous financial and operational actions.
Standardization of agent-to-agent communication protocols and interoperability.
Breakthrough in edge-compute efficiency allowing local, low-latency agent execution.
